decision.reason: peak L22 (GDN) is the contrastive hotspot; L23 (Gated Attn) is the downstream consolidation layer. Training SAE at L23 follows the Qwen3.5 pattern (peak L13 → SAE L18) and captures both signals.

key_observations: Qwen3.6 peak L22 sits between Qwen3.5-4B (L13) and Gemma4-E4B (L27) in relative depth (55%)
Qwen3.6 has unusually large peak-to-commit gap (17 layers) — MoE + thinking mode pushes final token prediction to near-last layer
Gated Attention layers are NOT Cohen's-d hotspots in Qwen3.6 — contra our prior hypothesis that GA bottlenecks would concentrate signal
Clean peak at L22 with 27% spread between min/max layer (vs 9% in v1 with truncated responses) — v2 reduced truncation is a viable protocol for hybrid models
